task_completion.description: Configure Stellarium location to Hernandez, New Mexico (36.0697 N, 106.1197 W).
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Stellarium Location window (F6) is open showing 'Hernandez' or the coordinates ~36.0697 N, ~106.1197 W, or the bottom status bar displays this location.

task_completion.description: Configure Stellarium date to November 1, 1941, and time to 23:49 UTC.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Stellarium Date and Time window (F5) or the bottom status bar shows the date '1941-11-01' and time '23:49' (with UTC timezone selected or implied).

task_completion.description: Enable Azimuthal Grid, Meridian Line, Atmosphere, and Landscape/Ground.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main Stellarium view displays a green azimuthal grid, a green meridian line, a lit sky (atmosphere), and the ground. The corresponding icons on the bottom toolbar should be highlighted/toggled on.

task_completion.description: Center the view on the Moon.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Moon is visible in the exact center of the Stellarium screen. It is likely selected, showing target crosshairs and astronomical information text in the top left corner.

task_completion.description: Capture a screenshot of the configured Stellarium view.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A screenshot notification appears in Stellarium (usually text at the bottom), or a file manager/terminal shows a new image file created in the screenshots directory.

task_completion.description: Persist the settings in the application configuration.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Stellarium Configuration window (F2) is open and the 'Save settings' button is clicked, or a terminal shows the agent modifying/saving the config.ini file.

task_completion.description: Write a report to ~/Desktop/moonrise_exhibit_notes.txt documenting the historical date, UTC time, geographic location, and confirmation that the Moon was above the horizon.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor is open showing a file named 'moonrise_exhibit_notes.txt' on the Desktop. The text explicitly mentions November 1, 1941, 23:49 UTC, Hernandez, NM (or the coordinates), and states that the Moon was above the horizon.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the Stellarium GUI to set up the scene.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Stellarium application open with its menus and dialogs being used, rather than the agent exclusively editing configuration files via terminal to bypass the visual setup.

integrity.description: The screenshot captured is from the actual Stellarium application session.
integrity.visual_evidence: The captured screenshot matches the scene configured by the agent in Stellarium, rather than being an image downloaded from a web browser.

integrity.description: The report is written in natural language documenting the findings, not just a direct copy-paste of the task description.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text in 'moonrise_exhibit_notes.txt' is formatted as a coherent note or report, synthesizing the requested data points rather than pasting the prompt verbatim.
